rationale: This Virtual Network is peered (an active, 'Connected' VNet Peering) to a Virtual Network in a DIFFERENT Azure subscription. A VNet Peering allows network traffic to flow directly between the two networks (subject to allow_virtual_network_access and any NSGs/route tables), effectively merging their address spaces from a connectivity standpoint. Cross-subscription connectivity is common and often intentional in hub-and-spoke architectures, so this is reported at low/informational severity to establish visibility over the tenant's network topology - review each entry and confirm it is an intended, documented connection rather than an unreviewed or forgotten one.
remediation: Confirm this peering is intentional and documented (e.g. a hub-spoke connectivity subscription). Remove any peering that is not needed. Where possible, restrict what the peering actually allows (disable allow_virtual_network_access / allow_forwarded_traffic / use_remote_gateways if not required) and use NSGs to further limit which traffic can actually cross the peering.
